perturbation.original_text: In this Agreement, "Confidential Information" means information disclosed by (or on behalf of) one party to the other party under this Agreement that is marked as confidential or, from its nature, content or the circumstances in which it is disclosed, might reasonably be supposed to be confidential, including the terms and conditions (including the Exhibits) of this Agreement. It does not include information that the recipient already knew, that becomes public through no fault of the recipient, that was independently developed by the recipient or that was lawfully given to the recipient by a third party.
perturbation.changed_text: In this Agreement, "Confidential Information" means any and all information exchanged between the parties, regardless of whether it is marked as confidential or could reasonably be supposed to be confidential. This includes, but is not limited to, the terms and conditions (including the Exhibits) of this Agreement, as well as information that the recipient already knew, that becomes public through no fault of the recipient, that was independently developed by the recipient or that was lawfully given to the recipient by a third party.
perturbation.explanation: This change broadens the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include virtually all information exchanged, regardless of whether it's explicitly marked or implicitly understood as confidential. The original definition excluded information already known to the recipient, publicly available information and independently developed information, but with this definition there is no exception on what can be considered confidential. This contradicts the intent of Clause 6, which aims to protect sensitive information and creates internal ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In this Agreement, "Confidential Information" means any and all information exchanged between the parties, regardless of whether it is marked as confidential or could reasonably be supposed to be confidential. This includes, but is not limited to, the terms and conditions (including the Exhibits) of this Agreement, as well as information that the recipient already knew, that becomes public through no fault of the recipient, that was independently developed by the recipient or that was lawfully given to the recipient by a third party. It does not include information that the recipient already knew, that becomes public through no fault of the recipient, that was independently developed by the recipient or that was lawfully given to the recipient by a third party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include *all* information exchanged, regardless of whether it is marked or reasonably understood as confidential. The contradiction arises from including 'information that the recipient already knew, that becomes public through no fault of the recipient, that was independently developed by the recipient or that was lawfully given to the recipient by a third party' within the definition of Confidential Information. By including these exceptions, the text contradicts itself by including and excluding the same information from the definition, making the definition and its application uncertain and unenforceable, which qualifies as an 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to Clause 9.1, this Agreement sets out all terms agreed between the parties in relation to its subject matter and supersedes all previous agreements between the parties relating to the same. In entering into this Agreement neither party has relied on any statement, representation or warranty not expressly set out in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to Clause 9.1, this Agreement sets out the primary terms agreed between the parties in relation to its subject matter and does not necessarily supersede all previous agreements between the parties relating to the same. In entering into this Agreement the parties acknowledge that they may have relied on statements, representations or warranties not expressly set out in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation undermines the "Entire Agreement" clause. Originally, the clause stated the agreement supersedes all previous agreements and that neither party relied on external representations. The changed text indicates the agreement only covers 'primary terms,' implies that prior agreements might still be relevant and admits that the parties could have relied on external statements, creating uncertainty about the comprehensive nature of the contract. With the added change of indicating that agreement does not necessarily supersede all previous agreements create an internal contradiction with the intended purpose of the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to Clause 9.1, this Agreement sets out the primary terms agreed between the parties in relation to its subject matter and does not necessarily supersede all previous agreements between the parties relating to the same. In entering into this Agreement the parties acknowledge that they may have relied on statements, representations or warranties not expressly set out in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent of the 'Entire Agreement' clause. By stating the agreement only covers 'primary terms' and that prior agreements might still be relevant, it undermines the clause's purpose of superseding all previous agreements. The acknowledgment that parties may have relied on external statements further weakens the clause's intended protection against claims based on representations not included in the contract. Because the changed text and the contradicted text are in the same location and the changed text modifies a prior agreement, making it so previous agreements are not necessarily superseded creates a direct and impactful contradiction.
